description: Machine-readable governance contract for SQLRustGo v2.8.0. Defines rules, baseline, invariants, and constraints for Proof-Carrying PR system.

R1.name: Test Immutability
R1.description: Test files define behavioral contracts. PR authors cannot modify them.

R2.name: Ignore Injection Prevention
R2.description: New #[ignore] annotations cannot be introduced by PR.
R2.grandfather_clause: Existing ignores from v2.7.0 are not checked

R3.name: Proof Authenticity
R3.description: Proof must be generated by CI, not submitted by PR.

R4.name: Full Execution Requirement
R4.description: All tests must run and pass. No skipped tests.
R4.check: cargo test exit code + result parsing

R5.name: Baseline Verification
R5.description: The baseline must be maintained exactly.

R6.name: Test Count Monotonicity
R6.rationale: Deleting tests is equivalent to ignoring them

R7.name: CI Workflow Integrity
R7.description: CI enforcement cannot be removed or modified by PR.
R7.invariant: INV7: CI enforcement is immutable relative to PR scope

validation_function.name: validate(PR)
validation_function.input: PR diff from origin/main to HEAD

INV1.description: Every test either passes, fails, or is ignored. No test is in an unknown state.

INV2.description: Proof must be generated by the verification engine in CI, not declared by PR.

INV3.description: The governance baseline is immutable. It defines the known-good state.

INV4.description: A PR merges if and only if it passes all validation rules.

INV5.description: Baseline verification is true exactly when all 226 tests pass with zero failures.

INV6.description: Passed test count never decreases below the baseline.

INV7.description: No PR can both modify CI and disable R1-R6 enforcement.

AV1.name: Test Immutability Bypass via Macro
AV1.method: Define #[test] in src/ procedural macro. Macro expands to generate test code at compile time. PR modifies src/, not tests/. R1 check passes.
AV1.remediation: R1 must check generated code, not just source (v2.9.0)

AV2.name: Partial Test Execution
AV2.method: Modify CI to run: cargo test --skip some_failing_test. Fewer tests run, fewer failures visible.
AV2.remediation: V must verify test count matches expected (v2.9.0)

AV3.name: CI Workflow Disablement
AV3.method: PR deletes .github/workflows/ci.yml. No CI runs, no enforcement.

AV4.name: Verification Engine Tampering
AV4.method: PR modifies verification_engine.py to always output: {passed: 226, failed: 0, baseline_verified: true}
AV4.note: V is in repository, not integrity-checked
AV4.remediation: V should be in separate protected repository (v2.9.0)

AV5.name: Semantic Regression
AV5.method: Change query processing logic (JOIN ordering, GROUP BY evaluation) without changing test assertions. Tests pass but results semantically wrong.
AV5.note: By design — behavior-based testing cannot detect semantic errors

AV6.name: Ignore Semantic Bypass
AV6.method: Replace #[ignore] with #[ignore = 'known issue']. R2 regex '^+.*#\[ignore\]' does not match.
AV6.remediation: Use AST parsing to detect any #[ignore] attribute (v2.9.0)

AV7.name: Baseline Poisoning
AV7.method: Use admin privileges to modify v2.8.0 baseline to: passed=100. Future PRs only need 100 tests.
AV7.remediation: Baseline must be in separate protected artifact store (v2.9.0)

AV8.name: Proof Replay Attack
AV8.method: Copy verification_report.json from successful CI run. Include in PR.
AV8.mitigated_by: R3 (CI always regenerates P, overwrites PR-submitted)
AV8.note: Attack fails because CI overwrites, not accepts

AV9.name: Non-Deterministic Test Masking
AV9.method: Test has race condition (fails 10%). Developer runs 5 times locally (all pass). CI runs once (passes). Production: intermittent failures.
AV9.remediation: CI must run tests multiple times (N-runs strategy, v2.9.0)

T2.assumption: cargo, rustc from official distribution are authentic

T3.assumption: 'test result:' format does not change across versions

T5.assumption: Repository settings block direct main push

T6.assumption: Admin users do not intentionally bypass governance

trust_chain.formula: Proof Valid ⇔ (T1 ∧ T2 ∧ T3 ∧ T4 ∧ T5 ∧ T6 ∧ C1)
trust_chain.break_at_any: If ANY assumption fails → Proof invalid

L1.name: Semantic Correctness Gap
L1.description: Tests verify behavioral properties, not semantic properties. A test passing means: code produces expected output for specific inputs. It does NOT mean: code is semantically correct according to relational algebra.
L1.consequence: AV5 (semantic regression) is undetectable by this system. This is not a bug. It is a design constraint.
L1.implication: SQLRustGo v2.8.0 proves procedural compliance, not semantic correctness.

L2.name: Trust Assumption Chain
L2.formal: Proof validity → T1 ∧ T2 ∧ T3 ∧ T4 ∧ T5 ∧ T6
L2.description: The entire proof chain rests on T1-T6. These are not technically verified within the system.
L2.consequence: If any T assumption is violated, proof is invalid. The system cannot detect its own compromise.

L3.name: CI Runner Compromise
L3.description: If GitHub Actions runner is compromised (T1), CI can report false results. Proof would appear valid while code is broken.
L3.consequence: No technical defense within the system. Requires organizational security controls.
L3.mitigation: Use GitHub's official runners (not self-hosted). Enable audit logging.

what_is_claimed: Proof P is CI-generated, not PR-submitted (R3)
what_is_not_claimed: Code is bug-free (tests pass ≠ no bugs)
Query results are semantically correct (behavior ≠ semantics)
CI environment is not compromised (trust assumption)
Test suite is complete (missing tests = missing coverage)
Future code will maintain baseline (only current state verified)

assurance_level.name: Proof-driven governance
assurance_level.description: Proof is CI-generated and tamper-evident. Tests are complete. Semantic correctness not verified.

proof_artifact_schema.baseline_verified: boolean — true iff passed=226 AND failed=0
